arguments.task: Visit official Slack and first-party pages to verify: 1) one-line product description from Slack homepage or official product page, 2) Slack API authentication methods from official Slack developer docs, including OAuth and token types if documented, 3) API surface types and breadth from official Slack developer docs, 4) rate limit documentation from official Slack docs, 5) pricing or access information showing whether Slack API access is self-serve or paid/gated, 6) whether Slack has an official MCP server or first-party MCP documentation, and 7) collect exact supporting URLs. Return a concise structured summary with fields: description, auth methods, auth notes, access tier, gating reason, api surface type and breadth, MCP exists and notes, rate limit notes, notable signals, evidence URLs.

raw_result.description: Slack is a work operating system for bringing people, projects, apps, and AI together.
raw_result.auth_notes: Slack developer docs include OAuth installation flows and multiple token types for API access, including bot, user, app-level, workflow, configuration, and service tokens. Evidence gathered indicates token-based bearer auth is central; exact support for every flow variant should be human-verified from the specific auth pages.
raw_result.gating_reason: Slack provides public developer documentation and self-serve app creation via its developer platform, with no partner-only or contact-sales gate identified in the gathered evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Public Slack developer docs show multiple API families including Web API and other platform APIs/events, indicating broad platform coverage for messaging, workspace apps, and administration; exact scope breadth should be human-verified.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party Slack MCP server or MCP documentation was verified in the gathered evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Slack documents Web API rate limits on an official rate limits page. Detailed tier values and method-specific quotas were not captured in the gathered evidence, so implementers should verify per-method limits directly from the rate-limit docs.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official Slack developer documentation is public and extensive, with dedicated sections for authentication, APIs, and rate limits. Multiple token classes suggest mature agent/tooling support but also scope-management complexity.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Access tier, API breadth characterization, MCP absence, and some auth/rate-limit specifics were inferred from partially gathered evidence without a final completed extraction from pricing/access or MCP-specific official pages.

result.description: Slack is a work operating system for bringing people, projects, apps, and AI together.
result.auth_notes: Slack developer docs include OAuth installation flows and multiple token types for API access, including bot, user, app-level, workflow, configuration, and service tokens. Evidence gathered indicates token-based bearer auth is central; exact support for every flow variant should be human-verified from the specific auth pages.
result.gating_reason: Slack provides public developer documentation and self-serve app creation via its developer platform, with no partner-only or contact-sales gate identified in the gathered evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Public Slack developer docs show multiple API families including Web API and other platform APIs/events, indicating broad platform coverage for messaging, workspace apps, and administration; exact scope breadth should be human-verified.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party Slack MCP server or MCP documentation was verified in the gathered evidence.

result.rate_limit_notes: Slack documents Web API rate limits on an official rate limits page. Detailed tier values and method-specific quotas were not captured in the gathered evidence, so implementers should verify per-method limits directly from the rate-limit docs.
result.notable_signals: Official Slack developer documentation is public and extensive, with dedicated sections for authentication, APIs, and rate limits. Multiple token classes suggest mature agent/tooling support but also scope-management complexity.
result.human_review_reason: Access tier, API breadth characterization, MCP absence, and some auth/rate-limit specifics were inferred from partially gathered evidence without a final completed extraction from pricing/access or MCP-specific official pages.
